methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $76M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Amca is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jai Malik and Eli Giovanetti that has raised $76.5M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable in the robotics and manufacturing sector, including later-stage companies such as 1X Technologies ($10.0B Series B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), and Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), alongside earlier-stage peers including Collaborative Robotics ($500M Series B) and Fairmat ($350M Series B).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using total raised ($76.5M) as a proxy for current value, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, implying $153M. The peer-median method across the six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within the robotics subsector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $130M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $614M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables drive the upper bound. Key uncertainties include the appropriate weighting of later-stage Series B peers relative to Amca's current stage, and the substantial valuation spread within the comparable set, which suggests material differences in technology maturity, market traction, or investor expectations across portfolio companies.
